_metadata.purpose: Explicitly allowed normalized term overlaps across routing databases. Any new overlap must be reviewed before scoring deployment.
_metadata.description: Explicitly allowed normalized term overlaps across routing databases. Any new overlap must be reviewed before scoring deployment.

allowed_overlaps.reason: Copper sulfate synonym; can appear as active mineral form while still treated as harmful additive in inactive context.
Irish moss source overlap; additive risk is context-dependent and should remain explicitly guarded.
May be listed as an active copper form in some labels; harmful classification applies when used as additive context.
Copper sulfate synonym with context-dependent routing.
Synthetic vitamin E form; retained in IQM for form mapping but tracked in harmful additives for transparency policy.
Sea moss overlap requires explicit policy guard to avoid accidental cross-file contamination.
Additive colorant overlap with iron nomenclature; explicitly allowlisted until data-model split is completed.
Synthetic folate synonym; context-aware policy overlap.
Policy overlap between synthetic-form transparency and active folate mapping.
Policy overlap between synthetic-form transparency and active vitamin E mapping.
Senna species synonym; FDA-approved OTC laxative with legitimate therapeutic use, but chronic overuse carries safety concerns (anthraquinone stimulant laxative).
Senna herb; dual-listed because it is a legitimate laxative supplement ingredient but also flagged for chronic-use safety concerns.
Senna species name; same dual-listing rationale as senna.
Senna extract form; same dual-listing rationale as senna.
Senna leaf form; same dual-listing rationale as senna.
Active glycosides in senna; same dual-listing rationale as senna.
Copper chlorophyllin is both a scorable supplement form and flagged in harmful additives as a synthetic colorant additive.
Copper chlorophyllin dual-listed: supplement form (IQM) and synthetic colorant (harmful additives).
SCC dual-listed: supplement form (IQM) and synthetic colorant (harmful additives).
Silicon dioxide is both a common tablet excipient/flow agent (harmful additives) and the chemical name for supplemental silica mineral (IQM).
